questions.stem: Under ASC 830, a foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is its local currency translates its financial statements using which method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Temporal method (remeasurement)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current rate method (translation)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Monetary/nonmonetary method

choices.label: D
choices.text: All items at historical rates

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-3, when the functional currency is the local (foreign) currency, the current rate method is used: assets and liabilities at the current exchange rate, equity at historical rates, and income statement items at weighted-average rates (or transaction-date rates). The resulting translation adjustment is reported in OCI (CTA).

questions.stem: A U.S. parent has a foreign subsidiary with net assets of LC 5,000,000 at January 1 (rate $0.80/LC). During the year, net income is LC 800,000 (average rate $0.75/LC). No dividends are paid. The December 31 rate is $0.72/LC. What is the translation adjustment (CTA) for the year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $424,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $600,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $424,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $176,000

questions.explanation: Ending net assets in LC = 5,000,000 + 800,000 = LC 5,800,000. Net assets translated at current rate = 5,800,000 Г— $0.72 = $4,176,000. Beginning net assets translated at beginning rate = 5,000,000 Г— $0.80 = $4,000,000. Net income translated at average rate = 800,000 Г— $0.75 = $600,000. Expected ending in USD = $4,000,000 + $600,000 = $4,600,000. CTA = Actual вҲ’ Expected = $4,176,000 вҲ’ $4,600,000 = вҲ’$424,000 (loss in OCI).

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary has the U.S. dollar as its functional currency. It holds a monetary asset of LC 1,000,000 when the rate is $0.50/LC. At year-end, the rate is $0.48/LC. What remeasurement gain or loss is recognized in earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $20,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $20,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $480,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No gain or loss; reported in OCI

questions.explanation: When the functional currency is the USD, the temporal method (remeasurement) is used. Monetary assets are remeasured at the current rate. Beginning value = LC 1,000,000 Г— $0.50 = $500,000. Ending value = LC 1,000,000 Г— $0.48 = $480,000. Loss = $500,000 вҲ’ $480,000 = $20,000. Remeasurement gains/losses are recognized in earnings (not OCI).

questions.stem: On November 1, a U.S. company sells goods to a French customer for вӮ¬200,000 on account. The spot rate is $1.15/вӮ¬. On December 31 (year-end), the spot rate is $1.18/вӮ¬. What foreign exchange gain or loss does the U.S. company recognize at year-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $6,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $6,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $236,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No gain or loss until collection

questions.explanation: The receivable was recorded at $1.15 Г— вӮ¬200,000 = $230,000. At year-end, the receivable is remeasured at $1.18 Г— вӮ¬200,000 = $236,000. Since the euro strengthened, the receivable is worth more in USD. Foreign exchange gain = $236,000 вҲ’ $230,000 = $6,000, recognized in earnings under ASC 830-20-35-1.
questions.explanationJa: еЈІжҺӣйҮ‘гҒҜ$1.15 Г— вӮ¬200,000 = $230,000гҒ§иЁҲдёҠгҖӮе№ҙжң«гҒ«$1.18 Г— вӮ¬200,000 = $236,000гҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгғҰгғјгғӯй«ҳгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠеЈІжҺӣйҮ‘гҒ®гғүгғ«жҸӣз®—йЎҚгҒҢеў—еҠ гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮзӮәжӣҝе·®зӣҠ = $236,000 - $230,000 = $6,000гӮ’еҪ“жңҹжҗҚзӣҠгҒ«иӘҚиӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: A company has net income of $2,400,000. It has 600,000 shares of common stock outstanding all year and 50,000 shares of 6%, $100 par cumulative preferred stock outstanding. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: Basic EPS = (Net income вҲ’ Preferred dividends) / Weighted-average common shares. Preferred dividends = 50,000 Г— $100 Г— 6% = $300,000. Basic EPS = ($2,400,000 вҲ’ $300,000) / 600,000 = $2,100,000 / 600,000 = $3.50.
questions.explanationJa: еҹәжң¬EPS = (зҙ”еҲ©зӣҠ - е„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“йҮ‘) / еҠ йҮҚе№іеқҮжҷ®йҖҡж ӘејҸж•°гҖӮзҙҜз©Қзҡ„е„Әе…Ҳж ӘгҒ®й…ҚеҪ“йҮ‘гҒҜе®ЈиЁҖгҒ®жңүз„ЎгҒ«й–ўдҝӮгҒӘгҒҸжҺ§йҷӨгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮе„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“йҮ‘ = 50,000ж Ә Г— $100 Г— 6% = $300,000гҖӮеҹәжң¬EPS = ($2,400,000 - $300,000) / 600,000 = $3.50гҖӮ

questions.stem: A company has basic EPS of $5.00 (net income $1,000,000, 200,000 WA shares). It has $2,000,000 of 8% convertible bonds (convertible into 50,000 common shares). The tax rate is 25%. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: If-converted method: Add back after-tax interest savings to numerator; add convertible shares to denominator. Interest = $2,000,000 Г— 8% = $160,000. Tax savings = $160,000 Г— 25% = $40,000. After-tax add-back = $160,000 вҲ’ $40,000 = $120,000. Diluted EPS = ($1,000,000 + $120,000) / (200,000 + 50,000) = $1,120,000 / 250,000 = $4.48. Since $4.48 < $5.00, the bonds are dilutive.
questions.explanationJa: гӮӨгғ•гғ»гӮігғігғҗгғјгғҶгғғгғүжі•пјҡеҲҶеӯҗгҒ«зЁҺеј•еҫҢеҲ©жҒҜзҜҖзҙ„йЎҚгӮ’еҠ з®—гҖҒеҲҶжҜҚгҒ«и»ўжҸӣж ӘејҸж•°гӮ’еҠ з®—гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮеҲ©жҒҜ = $2,000,000 Г— 8% = $160,000гҖӮзЁҺеҠ№жһң = $160,000 Г— 25% = $40,000гҖӮзЁҺеј•еҫҢеҠ з®—йЎҚ = $160,000 - $40,000 = $120,000гҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = ($1,000,000 + $120,000) / (200,000 + 50,000) = $4.48гҖӮ$4.48 < $5.00гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңгҒӮгӮҠгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Stock Options (Treasury Stock Method)
questions.stem: A company has net income of $800,000 and 400,000 weighted-average shares outstanding. It has 40,000 stock options with an exercise price of $20. The average market price during the year is $50. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Treasury stock method: Proceeds from exercise = 40,000 Г— $20 = $800,000. Shares repurchased at average market price = $800,000 / $50 = 16,000. Incremental shares = 40,000 вҲ’ 16,000 = 24,000. Diluted EPS = $800,000 / (400,000 + 24,000) = $800,000 / 424,000 = $1.89.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•пјҡиЎҢдҪҝгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢжү•иҫјйҮ‘ = 40,000 Г— $20 = $800,000гҖӮе№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгҒ§иІ·жҲ»гҒ— = $800,000 / $50 = 16,000ж ӘгҖӮеў—еҠ ж Әж•° = 40,000 - 16,000 = 24,000ж ӘгҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = $800,000 /пјҲ400,000 + 24,000пјү= $1.89гҖӮ

questions.stem: A company had 500,000 shares outstanding on January 1. On April 1, it issued 100,000 additional shares. On October 1, it repurchased 60,000 shares as treasury stock. What is the weighted-average number of shares for the year?
questions.explanationJa: жңҹй–“еҲҘгҒ®зҷәиЎҢжёҲж ӘејҸж•°пјҡ1жңҲ-3жңҲ 500,000ж ӘгҖҒ4жңҲ-9жңҲ 600,000ж ӘпјҲ+100,000зҷәиЎҢпјүгҖҒ10жңҲ-12жңҲ 540,000ж ӘпјҲ-60,000иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸеҸ–еҫ—пјүгҖӮеҠ йҮҚе№іеқҮ = (500,000 Г— 3/12) + (600,000 Г— 6/12) + (540,000 Г— 3/12) = 125,000 + 300,000 + 135,000 = 560,000ж ӘгҖӮ

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary (functional currency = local currency) reports depreciation expense of LC 400,000 for the year. The asset was acquired when the exchange rate was $0.90/LC. The average rate for the year is $0.85/LC. The year-end rate is $0.82/LC. At what dollar amount is depreciation reported on the translated income statement?
questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (translation), ALL income statement items are translated at the average rate for the period, regardless of the nature of the expense. Depreciation = LC 400,000 Г— $0.85 = $340,000. The historical rate ($0.90) is only used in the temporal method (remeasurement) for non-monetary items.

questions.stem: A company reported basic EPS of $4.00 for Year 1 (net income $800,000; 200,000 WA shares). On March 1, Year 2, the company declares a 10% stock dividend. What is the restated Year 1 basic EPS for comparative purposes?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-12, stock dividends and stock splits are applied retroactively to all prior periods presented for EPS calculations. Adjusted shares = 200,000 Г— 1.10 = 220,000. Restated Year 1 EPS = $800,000 / 220,000 = $3.636 вүҲ $3.64.
questions.explanationJa: ж ӘејҸй…ҚеҪ“гғ»ж ӘејҸеҲҶеүІгҒҜEPSиЁҲз®—гҒ§е…ЁгҒҰгҒ®йҒҺеҺ»жңҹй–“гҒ«йҒЎеҸҠйҒ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮиӘҝж•ҙеҫҢж Әж•° = 200,000 Г— 1.10 = 220,000ж ӘгҖӮдҝ®жӯЈеҶҚиЎЁзӨәеҫҢгҒ®Year 1 EPS = $800,000 / 220,000 = $3.64гҖӮ

questions.stem: Under ASC 830, which of the following factors is most relevant in determining a foreign subsidiary's functional currency?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The currency in which the subsidiary primarily generates and expends cash

choices.label: B
choices.text: The currency of the parent company

choices.label: C
choices.text: The currency in which the subsidiary's stock is traded

choices.label: D
choices.text: The currency with the lowest inflation rate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-55-5, the functional currency is determined by analyzing the economic environment in which the entity primarily generates and expends cash. Key indicators include: currency of sales prices, markets, labor costs, materials, financing, and intercompany transactions. It is a factual determination, not a free choice.

questions.stem: Under ASC 830, if a foreign subsidiary operates in a highly inflationary economy (cumulative inflation of approximately 100% or more over 3 years), which reporting currency method is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The reporting currency (U.S. dollar) is used as the functional currency, requiring remeasurement

choices.label: B
choices.text: The local currency remains the functional currency with translation

choices.label: C
choices.text: A stable third-party currency must be designated as the functional currency

choices.label: D
choices.text: Financial statements are presented in the local currency without conversion

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-11, if a foreign entity operates in a highly inflationary environment (approximately 100% cumulative inflation over 3 years), the financial statements must be remeasured as if the functional currency were the reporting currency (USD). This prevents distortion from using a rapidly depreciating currency.

questions.subtopic: Foreign Currency Transaction - Hedged vs Unhedged
questions.stem: On September 1, a U.S. company purchases inventory from a Japanese supplier for ВҘ10,000,000 on account (spot rate $0.0090/ВҘ). The company hedges with a forward contract at $0.0092/ВҘ. At December 31, the spot rate is $0.0095/ВҘ. What exchange loss does the company report on the unhedged payable at year-end?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because the payable is hedged

questions.explanation: The payable is remeasured at the spot rate regardless of hedging. Payable at transaction date = ВҘ10,000,000 Г— $0.0090 = $90,000. Payable at year-end = ВҘ10,000,000 Г— $0.0095 = $95,000. Exchange loss = $95,000 вҲ’ $90,000 = $5,000. The hedge gain on the forward contract would offset this loss, but the payable remeasurement itself shows a $5,000 loss.

questions.stem: Under the current rate method, at which exchange rate are the following items translated: (1) land acquired 5 years ago, (2) accounts payable, and (3) common stock issued at incorporation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Land: current rate; A/P: current rate; Common stock: historical rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Land: historical rate; A/P: current rate; Common stock: historical rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Land: current rate; A/P: historical rate; Common stock: current rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: All three at the current rate

questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (ASC 830-10-45-3): All assets (including land) and all liabilities are translated at the current (year-end) exchange rate. Equity accounts (common stock, APIC) are translated at historical rates. This differs from the temporal method, where nonmonetary assets like land use historical rates.

questions.stem: Under the temporal method (remeasurement), a foreign subsidiary has the following balances in local currency: Cash LC 500,000; Inventory (cost) LC 800,000; Land LC 1,200,000; Accounts Payable LC 600,000. Which items are remeasured at the current exchange rate?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash and Accounts Payable only

choices.label: B
choices.text: All four items

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cash, Inventory, and Accounts Payable

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cash and Land only

questions.explanation: Under the temporal method, monetary items (cash, receivables, payables) are remeasured at the current rate. Nonmonetary items carried at cost (inventory at cost, land, PPE) are remeasured at historical rates. Nonmonetary items carried at fair value (e.g., inventory at NRV) would use the rate when fair value was determined.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent has a long-term intercompany loan receivable from its foreign subsidiary denominated in the subsidiary's local currency. The loan is considered part of the net investment. How is the exchange gain/loss on this loan reported in the parent's consolidated financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) within OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: In current period earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to goodwill

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not recognized until settlement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-20-35-3, foreign currency transaction gains/losses on intercompany transactions that are of a long-term investment nature (i.e., settlement is not planned or anticipated) are reported in the CTA component of OCI in the consolidated statements, similar to translation adjustments.

questions.subtopic: Forward Contract - Hedge of Net Investment
questions.stem: A U.S. parent enters a forward contract to sell ВЈ5,000,000 to hedge its net investment in a British subsidiary. At year-end, the forward contract has a gain of $120,000 (effective portion $115,000, ineffective portion $5,000). Under ASC 815, how are these amounts reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Effective portion $115,000 in CTA (OCI); ineffective portion $5,000 in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entire $120,000 in CTA (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Entire $120,000 in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Effective portion $115,000 in earnings; ineffective portion $5,000 in OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-35-35-1, for a net investment hedge using a derivative, the effective portion ($115,000) is recorded in CTA within OCI. The ineffective portion ($5,000) is recognized immediately in earnings. This is consistent with the treatment of other hedging relationships.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent recognizes a $200,000 cumulative translation loss in OCI from translating a foreign subsidiary. The tax rate is 25%. What is the deferred tax effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $50,000 recognized in OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: No deferred tax effect because translation adjustments are permanent

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax liability of $50,000 in earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: A deferred tax asset of $50,000 in earnings

questions.explanation: Translation adjustments in OCI create temporary differences that result in deferred tax effects. A CTA loss creates a deferred tax asset = $200,000 Г— 25% = $50,000. Under ASC 740-20-45-11, the deferred tax effect of items in OCI is also reported in OCI (intraperiod tax allocation).

questions.stem: A company has 300,000 weighted-average shares, net income of $600,000, and 20,000 stock options with an exercise price of $60. The average market price is $45. What effect do the options have on diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The options are anti-dilutive and excluded from diluted EPS

choices.label: B
choices.text: The options add 20,000 shares to the denominator

choices.label: C
choices.text: The options add 6,667 shares to the denominator

choices.label: D
choices.text: The options reduce net income by the option premium

questions.explanation: Under the treasury stock method, options are anti-dilutive when the exercise price exceeds the average market price (out-of-the-money). Here, exercise price $60 > market price $45, so the assumed repurchase of shares would exceed shares issued, resulting in a net decrease in shares (anti-dilutive). Anti-dilutive securities are excluded from diluted EPS.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒиЎҢдҪҝдҫЎж јгҒҢе№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮӢпјҲгӮўгӮҰгғҲгғ»гӮӘгғ–гғ»гӮ¶гғ»гғһгғҚгғјпјүгӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғігҒҜйҖҶеёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„гҖӮиЎҢдҪҝдҫЎж ј$60 > еёӮе ҙдҫЎж ј$45гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒиІ·жҲ»гҒ—ж Әж•°гҒҢзҷәиЎҢж Әж•°гӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮҠгҖҒж Әж•°гҒҢзҙ”жёӣгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮйҖҶеёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„гҒӘиЁјеҲёгҒҜеёҢи–„еҢ–EPSгҒӢгӮүйҷӨеӨ–гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: A company has 100,000 common shares and 20,000 participating preferred shares that participate in dividends on a 1:1 basis with common. Net income is $360,000 and no dividends are declared. Under the two-class method, what is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: Under the two-class method (ASC 260-10-45-60A), undistributed earnings are allocated to each class based on participation rights. Total shares = 100,000 + 20,000 = 120,000. Common allocation = $360,000 Г— (100,000/120,000) = $300,000. Basic EPS = $300,000 / 100,000 = $3.00.

questions.stem: A company has net income of $500,000 and 200,000 weighted-average common shares. It has 10,000 shares of noncumulative preferred stock ($100 par, 5% dividend). No preferred dividends were declared during the year. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: For noncumulative preferred stock, only declared dividends are subtracted from net income in the EPS calculation. Since no dividends were declared, the full net income is available to common shareholders. Basic EPS = $500,000 / 200,000 = $2.50. This differs from cumulative preferred, where dividends are deducted regardless of declaration.

questions.stem: A company has net income of $1,200,000 and 500,000 weighted-average shares. It has 50,000 warrants with an exercise price of $30. The average market price is $50. What are the incremental shares from the warrants?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 20,000 shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: 50,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 30,000 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 15,000 shares

questions.explanation: Treasury stock method: Proceeds = 50,000 Г— $30 = $1,500,000. Shares repurchased = $1,500,000 / $50 = 30,000. Incremental shares = 50,000 вҲ’ 30,000 = 20,000. Since exercise price ($30) < market price ($50), the warrants are dilutive.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•пјҡжү•иҫјйҮ‘ = 50,000 Г— $30 = $1,500,000гҖӮиІ·жҲ»гҒ—ж Әж•° = $1,500,000 / $50 = 30,000ж ӘгҖӮеў—еҠ ж Әж•° = 50,000 - 30,000 = 20,000ж ӘгҖӮиЎҢдҪҝдҫЎж ј$30 < еёӮе ҙдҫЎж ј$50гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңгҒӮгӮҠгҖӮ

questions.stem: Under ASC 260, which companies are required to present diluted EPS on the face of the income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Public companies with complex capital structures (those with potentially dilutive securities)

choices.label: B
choices.text: All companies, whether public or private

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only companies with market capitalization above $100 million

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only companies with outstanding convertible debt

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-2, entities with publicly traded common stock or potential common stock must present both basic and diluted EPS on the face of the income statement. Private companies are not required to present EPS. A complex capital structure includes convertible securities, options, warrants, or other potentially dilutive instruments.

questions.stem: A company reports income from continuing operations of $800,000 and a loss from discontinued operations of $200,000. It has 400,000 weighted-average shares and no preferred stock. How should EPS be presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: EPS from continuing operations $2.00; EPS from discontinued operations $(0.50); total EPS $1.50

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only total EPS of $1.50

choices.label: C
choices.text: EPS from continuing operations $2.00 only

choices.label: D
choices.text: EPS from continuing operations $1.50; no separate discontinued operations EPS

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-3, EPS must be presented for income from continuing operations AND for net income on the face of the income statement. EPS for discontinued operations must also be presented either on the face or in the notes. Continuing: $800,000/400,000 = $2.00. Discontinued: -$200,000/400,000 = -$0.50. Total: $1.50.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent sells inventory to its British subsidiary for ВЈ500,000 when the rate is $1.30/ВЈ. This is a short-term trade receivable (settlement expected within 60 days). At year-end, the rate is $1.35/ВЈ. How is the exchange gain/loss reported in the consolidated statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Exchange gain of $25,000 in earnings (the intra-entity balance is eliminated but the exchange effect is retained)

choices.label: B
choices.text: No exchange effect because intra-entity transactions are eliminated

choices.label: C
choices.text: Exchange gain of $25,000 in OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Exchange gain deferred until the inventory is sold to third parties

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-20-35-1, exchange gains/losses on intercompany foreign currency transactions that are of a trading nature (short-term) are included in earnings even in consolidated statements. The intercompany balances are eliminated, but the exchange gain/loss is real and remains. Gain = ВЈ500,000 Г— ($1.35 вҲ’ $1.30) = $25,000.

questions.stem: A U.S. company has a 30% equity method investment in a Japanese company (functional currency = yen). How does the U.S. company report the translation adjustment from the investee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The investor's proportionate share of the investee's CTA is reported in the investor's OCI

choices.label: B
choices.text: The translation adjustment is reported in the investor's earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: No translation adjustment is reported; the investment is carried at cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: The full CTA of the investee is included in the investor's OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-19, when an investor applies the equity method to a foreign investee, the investor recognizes its proportionate share of the investee's translation adjustment in OCI. Only the investor's percentage (30%) of the CTA is included, not the full amount.

questions.stem: Where are remeasurement gains and losses reported on the income statement under ASC 830?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included in determining net income for the period, typically in other income/expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: In other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Below net income as a separate item

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an extraordinary item

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-17, remeasurement gains and losses (from the temporal method) are included in determining net income for the period. They are typically reported in other income/expense or as a separate line item within the income statement, not in OCI.

questions.subtopic: Basic EPS - Multiple Classes of Common
questions.stem: A company has two classes of common stock: Class A (400,000 shares, full voting and dividend rights) and Class B (100,000 shares, entitled to 50% of per-share dividends of Class A). Net income is $600,000. No dividends are declared. What is Class A basic EPS under the two-class method?
questions.explanation: Under the two-class method, undistributed earnings are allocated based on participation rights. Class B gets 50% per share vs Class A: equivalent shares = 100,000 Г— 0.5 = 50,000. Total equivalent shares = 400,000 + 50,000 = 450,000. Class A allocation = $600,000 Г— (400,000/450,000) = $533,333. EPS = $533,333/400,000 = $1.33.

questions.subtopic: EPS - Retrospective Adjustment for Stock Split
questions.stem: A company had 200,000 shares outstanding all of Year 1 and reported EPS of $5.00. On July 1, Year 2, the company executes a 2-for-1 stock split. How is Year 1 EPS restated for comparative presentation?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-12, stock splits are applied retroactively to all prior periods for EPS computation. Year 1 adjusted shares = 200,000 Г— 2 = 400,000. Restated Year 1 EPS = $1,000,000 (net income) / 400,000 = $2.50. The net income numerator does not change.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary's functional currency changes from the local currency to the U.S. dollar due to a change in economic circumstances. Under ASC 830, how is this change accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospectively from the date of change; translated balances at the change date become the new accounting basis

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retroactively by restating all prior periods

choices.label: C
choices.text: The CTA balance is immediately reclassified to earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: The subsidiary must prepare completely new opening balances

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-7, a change in functional currency is accounted for prospectively. The translated amounts for nonmonetary assets at the date of change become the historical cost basis for subsequent remeasurement. The CTA balance in AOCI at the change date remains in equity until disposal of the subsidiary.

questions.stem: When translating the financial statements of a foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is the local currency, which exchange rate is used for fixed assets on the balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Historical rate at the date of acquisition

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average rate for the current period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Forward rate for the next reporting period

questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (ASC 830-10-45-3), when the functional currency is the local currency, ALL assets and liabilities are translated at the current exchange rate at the balance sheet date. This includes fixed assets. The historical rate method applies only under remeasurement (when the functional currency differs from the local currency), where nonmonetary assets like fixed assets use historical rates.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent has a subsidiary in Brazil. The subsidiary's books are maintained in Brazilian reais, but its functional currency is the U.S. dollar. Which method should be used to convert the subsidiary's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Remeasurement (temporal method)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Translation (current rate method)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Either method, at the parent's election

choices.label: D
choices.text: No conversion is needed since the parent reports in USD

questions.explanation: When the functional currency differs from the local currency (books in reais but functional currency is USD), remeasurement using the temporal method is required (ASC 830-10-45-17). Monetary items are remeasured at current rates, nonmonetary items at historical rates, and the resulting gains/losses are reported in earnings. Translation (current rate method) is used only when the functional currency IS the local currency.

questions.stem: Orion Corp. has a cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) balance of $250,000 related to its German subsidiary. Orion sells 100% of the subsidiary. How should Orion account for the CTA?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reclassify the entire $250,000 from AOCI to earnings as part of the gain or loss on disposal

choices.label: B
choices.text: Leave the $250,000 in AOCI permanently

choices.label: C
choices.text: Amortize the $250,000 over 5 years beginning from the sale date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transfer the $250,000 directly to retained earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-30-40-1, upon the sale or substantially complete liquidation of an investment in a foreign entity, the CTA balance associated with that entity is reclassified from AOCI into earnings as part of the gain or loss on disposal. A complete sale of 100% of the subsidiary triggers full release of the CTA to earnings.

questions.stem: Under the temporal method of remeasurement, where are foreign currency remeasurement gains and losses reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In current period earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: In other comprehensive income (OCI)

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct adjustment to stockholders' equity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred and amortized over the remaining asset life

questions.explanation: Under the temporal method (remeasurement), gains and losses arising from the remeasurement process are reported in current period earnings (ASC 830-10-45-18). This contrasts with translation under the current rate method, where the translation adjustment (CTA) is reported in OCI. The distinction is fundamental: remeasurement gains/losses go to earnings; translation adjustments go to OCI.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary operates in a highly inflationary economy (cumulative inflation exceeding 100% over 3 years). Regardless of the subsidiary's actual functional currency, how must the parent account for the subsidiary's financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Remeasure the statements as if the functional currency were the parent's reporting currency

choices.label: B
choices.text: Translate using the current rate method with an inflation adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use historical cost financial statements without any currency conversion

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restate the subsidiary's statements for inflation before translating

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-11, if a foreign entity operates in a highly inflationary economy (cumulative inflation of approximately 100% or more over a 3-year period), its financial statements must be remeasured as if the functional currency were the reporting currency of the parent (e.g., USD). This effectively treats the parent's currency as the functional currency, using the temporal method. The rationale is that translation at current rates in a highly inflationary environment would produce meaningless results.

questions.stem: On November 1, Year 1, Mason Corp. (USD functional currency) purchases goods from a UK supplier for ВЈ100,000, payable on January 31, Year 2. The exchange rates are: Nov 1 = $1.30/ВЈ, Dec 31 = $1.35/ВЈ, Jan 31 = $1.32/ВЈ. What is the foreign currency transaction gain or loss recognized in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $5,000 loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $5,000 gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $2,000 gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: $3,000 loss

questions.explanation: At November 1, the payable is recorded at ВЈ100,000 Г— $1.30 = $130,000. At December 31 (year-end), the payable is remeasured at ВЈ100,000 Г— $1.35 = $135,000. The increase in the USD-equivalent payable of $5,000 ($135,000 вҲ’ $130,000) is a foreign currency transaction loss recognized in Year 1 earnings, because it will cost more dollars to settle the liability.

questions.stem: Pine Corp. had 500,000 shares of common stock outstanding for the entire year. On July 1, Pine repurchased 40,000 shares of treasury stock. Net income was $1,200,000. What is Pine's basic EPS?
questions.explanation: Basic EPS = Net income Г· Weighted-average shares outstanding. The weighted-average shares: 500,000 for JanвҖ“Jun (6/12) + 460,000 for JulвҖ“Dec (6/12) = 250,000 + 230,000 = 480,000. Basic EPS = $1,200,000 Г· 480,000 = $2.50. Treasury stock repurchases reduce the shares outstanding from the repurchase date, weighted for the fraction of the year.

questions.stem: Sage Inc. has 200,000 shares of common stock outstanding and 10,000 shares of convertible preferred stock. Each preferred share is convertible into 3 common shares and pays an annual dividend of $5 per share. Net income is $800,000. What is diluted EPS using the if-converted method?
questions.explanation: If-converted method: Numerator = Net income (no preferred dividend deduction since assumed converted) = $800,000. Denominator = 200,000 + (10,000 Г— 3) = 230,000 shares. Diluted EPS = $800,000 Г· 230,000 = $3.478, rounded to $3.48. Compare with basic EPS = ($800,000 вҲ’ $50,000) Г· 200,000 = $3.75. Since diluted EPS ($3.48) < basic EPS ($3.75), the conversion is dilutive and is included.

questions.stem: Fern Corp. has basic EPS of $3.00. Fern has outstanding convertible bonds that, if converted, would increase the numerator by $120,000 (after-tax interest) and the denominator by 50,000 shares. Fern also has stock options that would add 10,000 incremental shares under the treasury stock method. In what order should these securities be evaluated for inclusion in diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stock options first (most dilutive per-share impact), then convertible bonds

choices.label: B
choices.text: Convertible bonds first because they affect the numerator

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both simultaneously in a single computation

choices.label: D
choices.text: In reverse chronological order of issuance

questions.explanation: ASC 260-10-45-21 through 45-44 requires potentially dilutive securities to be evaluated in order from most dilutive to least dilutive (the incremental approach). Options/warrants (which have no numerator impact, so per-share effect = $0/share) are always the most dilutive and are entered first. Then convertible securities are ranked by their incremental EPS effect ($120,000 Г· 50,000 = $2.40/share). Each security is included only if it is dilutive relative to the cumulative diluted EPS at that point.

questions.stem: Lux Corp. reported basic EPS of $4.00 for Year 1 based on 100,000 weighted-average shares. In March of Year 2, Lux declares a 10% stock dividend. What is the restated basic EPS for Year 1 in comparative financial statements?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-55-12, stock dividends and stock splits are given retroactive treatment for all periods presented. The Year 1 weighted-average shares must be adjusted: 100,000 Г— 1.10 = 110,000. Restated EPS = $400,000 (original net income: $4.00 Г— 100,000) Г· 110,000 = $3.636, rounded to $3.64. This ensures comparability across periods.

questions.stem: Under the current rate method (ASC 830), which exchange rate is used to translate revenue and expense items of a foreign subsidiary?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The weighted-average exchange rate for the period

choices.label: B
choices.text: The current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The historical rate at the date of each transaction

choices.label: D
choices.text: The spot rate at the beginning of the period

questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (ASC 830-10-45-3), revenues and expenses are translated at the exchange rate at the dates those items were recognized, but for practical purposes, a weighted-average rate for the period is commonly used as an approximation. The current (closing) rate is used only for balance sheet items.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary has a functional currency that differs from its local currency. Under the temporal method, at what rate should inventory carried at cost be remeasured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Historical rate when the inventory was acquired

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average rate for the period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Forward rate as of the expected sale date

questions.explanation: Under the temporal method (ASC 830-10-45-18), nonmonetary assets carried at historical costвҖ”including inventory at costвҖ”are remeasured using the historical exchange rate at the date of acquisition. If inventory is carried at market (NRV under lower of cost or market), the current rate would be used. The key principle is that the rate matches the measurement basis of the item.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent sells inventory to its Canadian subsidiary (functional currency: Canadian dollar) for C$500,000 when the exchange rate is $0.80/C$. The parent's cost was US$350,000. In preparing consolidated financial statements, what amount of intercompany profit must be eliminated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 (the parent's profit measured in USD)

choices.label: B
choices.text: C$62,500 (the profit measured in Canadian dollars)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $400,000 (the total selling price in USD)

choices.label: D
choices.text: No elimination is needed for foreign currency transactions

questions.explanation: The intercompany profit is computed in the selling entity's currency (USD). The parent sold for C$500,000 Г— $0.80 = US$400,000, with a cost of US$350,000, yielding profit of US$50,000. This profit is eliminated in consolidation. The elimination is measured in the parent's functional currency (USD) because that is the currency in which the profit was generated.

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS Treasury Stock Method Calculation
questions.stem: Nova Inc. has 300,000 shares outstanding and net income of $600,000. Nova has 20,000 stock options with an exercise price of $15. The average market price during the year was $25. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Treasury stock method: Proceeds = 20,000 Г— $15 = $300,000. Shares repurchased at market = $300,000 Г· $25 = 12,000. Incremental shares = 20,000 вҲ’ 12,000 = 8,000. Diluted shares = 300,000 + 8,000 = 308,000. Diluted EPS = $600,000 Г· 308,000 = $1.948, rounded to $1.94. Since the exercise price ($15) is below the market price ($25), the options are dilutive.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•пјҡжү•иҫјйҮ‘йЎҚ=20,000Г—$15=$300,000гҖӮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгҒ§иІ·жҲ»гҒ—=$300,000Г·$25=12,000ж ӘгҖӮеў—еҠ ж ӘејҸж•°=20,000вҲ’12,000=8,000ж ӘгҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–еҫҢж ӘејҸж•°=308,000ж ӘгҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS=$600,000Г·308,000=$1.94гҖӮ

questions.stem: West Corp. has net income of $500,000. It has 10,000 shares of cumulative preferred stock ($100 par, 6% dividend rate) and 200,000 shares of common stock outstanding. No preferred dividends were declared in the current year. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: For cumulative preferred stock, preferred dividends are deducted from net income in computing basic EPS regardless of whether declared. Preferred dividend = 10,000 Г— $100 Г— 6% = $60,000. Basic EPS = ($500,000 вҲ’ $60,000) Г· 200,000 = $440,000 Г· 200,000 = $2.20. For noncumulative preferred, only declared dividends are deducted.

questions.stem: Under the current rate method, at what rate are equity accounts (other than retained earnings) translated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Historical rates at the dates of the respective equity transactions

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average rate for the period

choices.label: D
choices.text: A blended rate based on the weighted-average of all equity issuances

questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (ASC 830-10-45-3), equity accounts such as common stock and additional paid-in capital are translated at historical ratesвҖ”the exchange rates in effect at the dates of the equity transactions (issuance of stock, etc.). Retained earnings is a derived amount (beginning retained earnings plus translated net income minus translated dividends). The cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) captures the residual balancing amount.

questions.stem: Grant Corp.'s compensation agreement provides that its CEO will receive 50,000 additional shares if the company's net income exceeds $10 million for any year. Year 1 net income is $12 million with 1,000,000 shares outstanding. How are these contingently issuable shares treated in diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Include the 50,000 shares in the denominator because the condition is currently met

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exclude them because the shares have not been issued

choices.label: C
choices.text: Include them only if the CEO has exercised the option

choices.label: D
choices.text: Include half the shares as a probability-weighted estimate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-48, contingently issuable shares are included in the computation of diluted EPS if the conditions for their issuance are currently satisfied (i.e., all necessary conditions have been met as of the end of the period). Since Year 1 net income of $12 million exceeds the $10 million threshold, the condition is met, and the 50,000 shares are included in the denominator.

questions.stem: Marsh Inc. has 100,000 common shares and 20,000 participating preferred shares. The preferred shares participate equally with common in undistributed earnings. Net income is $360,000 and preferred dividends of $40,000 are declared. What is basic EPS for common shareholders using the two-class method?
questions.explanation: Two-class method: (1) Deduct preferred dividends: $360,000 вҲ’ $40,000 = $320,000 undistributed. (2) Allocate undistributed earnings based on participation rights: Common gets 100,000/(100,000+20,000) Г— $320,000 = $266,667. (3) Basic EPS = $266,667 Г· 100,000 = $2.667, rounded to $2.67. The two-class method is required when securities participate in dividends with common stock.

questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, a U.S. company purchases inventory from a German supplier for вӮ¬200,000 when the exchange rate is $1.12/вӮ¬. The company pays on December 31, Year 1, when the rate is $1.08/вӮ¬. What foreign currency transaction gain or loss does the U.S. company recognize?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $8,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $8,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $4,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $4,000

questions.explanation: On October 1, the payable is recorded at вӮ¬200,000 Г— $1.12 = $224,000. On December 31, settlement requires вӮ¬200,000 Г— $1.08 = $216,000. The U.S. company pays $216,000 to settle a $224,000 payable, resulting in a gain of $224,000 вҲ’ $216,000 = $8,000. When the dollar strengthens (fewer dollars per euro), a company with a foreign-currency-denominated payable recognizes a gain. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a U.S. company enters into a 12-month forward contract to buy ВЈ500,000 at a forward rate of $1.35/ВЈ to hedge a firm commitment. The spot rate on January 1 is $1.30/ВЈ. The company elects to exclude the forward points from the hedge effectiveness assessment under ASC 815. What is the total forward premium to be amortized over the contract period?
questions.explanation: The forward premium is the difference between the forward rate and the spot rate multiplied by the notional amount. Forward premium = (Forward rate вҲ’ Spot rate) Г— Notional = ($1.35 вҲ’ $1.30) Г— ВЈ500,000 = $0.05 Г— 500,000 = $25,000. Under ASC 815-20-25-83A, when a company elects to exclude forward points from hedge effectiveness, the premium is amortized to earnings systematically over the life of the contract. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: A U.S. company designates a forward contract as a fair value hedge of a firm commitment to purchase equipment for ВҘ100,000,000. At the hedge inception, the forward rate is $0.0090/ВҘ. At year-end, the forward rate changes to $0.0095/ВҘ. Under ASC 815, where is the gain on the firm commitment recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In earnings as a foreign currency gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: In OCI as a hedging adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an adjustment to the basis of the equipment when acquired

choices.label: D
choices.text: In earnings, offset by the loss on the forward contract

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-25-35-8, in a fair value hedge of a firm commitment, the gain or loss on the hedging instrument (forward contract) and the offsetting loss or gain on the firm commitment attributable to the hedged risk are both recognized currently in earnings. The forward contract has a loss of ВҘ100,000,000 Г— ($0.0095 вҲ’ $0.0090) = $50,000 (company must pay more dollars). The firm commitment has an offsetting gain of $50,000. Both are recognized in earnings, with the net effect near zero if the hedge is effective. The correct answer is D.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary's retained earnings in USD at January 1, Year 3, are $840,000. During Year 3, the subsidiary reports net income of LC 600,000 (translated at the average rate of $0.82/LC) and declares dividends of LC 100,000 (rate on declaration date: $0.80/LC). What are the subsidiary's translated retained earnings at December 31, Year 3?
questions.explanation: Translated retained earnings are computed using the retained earnings rollforward approach (not by applying the current rate): Beginning RE (USD) + Translated net income вҲ’ Translated dividends = $840,000 + (LC 600,000 Г— $0.82) вҲ’ (LC 100,000 Г— $0.80) = $840,000 + $492,000 вҲ’ $80,000 = $1,252,000. Under ASC 830-10-45-3, retained earnings are not translated at any single rate but result from the accumulation of prior translated amounts. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: Under ASC 830, when translating the financial statements of a foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is its local currency, at what exchange rate is goodwill arising from the acquisition of the subsidiary translated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Historical rate at the date of acquisition

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average rate for the current year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Lower of historical rate or current rate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-17 and ASC 830-30-45-3, when the functional currency is the local currency (current rate method / translation), all assets and liabilities, including goodwill, are translated at the current exchange rate at the balance sheet date. Goodwill is only translated at the historical rate under the temporal method (remeasurement), when the functional currency is the reporting currency. The correct answer is B.

questions.stem: Under the current rate method (translation), how should accumulated depreciation on a foreign subsidiary's equipment be translated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Historical rate when the related asset was acquired

choices.label: B
choices.text: Average rate for each year depreciation was recorded

choices.label: C
choices.text: Current exchange rate at the balance sheet date

choices.label: D
choices.text: Rate in effect when each depreciation charge was recognized

questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (ASC 830-10-45-3), all assets and liabilities are translated at the current exchange rate at the balance sheet date. Accumulated depreciation is a contra-asset and follows the same translation rule as the related asset. Under translation, both the gross asset and accumulated depreciation are translated at the current rate. The historical rate treatment applies only under the temporal method (remeasurement). The correct answer is C.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary uses the U.S. dollar as its functional currency. The subsidiary prepaid LC 300,000 for rent on March 1, Year 1, when the exchange rate was $0.65/LC. At December 31, Year 1, the current rate is $0.70/LC and the average rate is $0.68/LC. LC 200,000 of the prepaid rent has been amortized. At what amount should the remaining prepaid rent of LC 100,000 be reported in the remeasured balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under the temporal method (remeasurement), prepaid expenses are nonmonetary assets and are remeasured at the historical exchange rate in effect when the prepaid was initially recorded. The remaining prepaid rent of LC 100,000 is remeasured at the historical rate of $0.65/LC = $65,000. The current rate and average rate are not used for nonmonetary assets under remeasurement. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary has the U.S. dollar as its functional currency. On June 1, Year 1, the subsidiary received LC 500,000 in advance for services to be performed over 10 months (JuneвҖ“March). The exchange rate on June 1 was $0.50/LC. At December 31, Year 1, the current rate is $0.55/LC. Under the temporal method, at what USD amount should the remaining deferred revenue (LC 200,000) be reported?
questions.explanation: Under the temporal method, deferred revenue (unearned revenue) is a nonmonetary liability because it represents an obligation to deliver goods or services rather than to pay a fixed amount of currency. Nonmonetary items are remeasured at the historical rate in effect when the transaction occurred. Remaining deferred revenue = LC 200,000 Г— $0.50 (historical rate) = $100,000. The current rate of $0.55 does not apply to nonmonetary liabilities. The correct answer is B.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary generates 60% of its revenue from local customers in local currency and 40% from exports priced in U.S. dollars. Its operating expenses are primarily incurred in local currency, and its financing is primarily from local banks in local currency. However, sales prices are strongly influenced by worldwide commodity prices denominated in U.S. dollars. Under ASC 830, which statement best describes the functional currency determination?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The functional currency is automatically the U.S. dollar because commodity prices are in dollars

choices.label: B
choices.text: The functional currency is automatically the local currency because the majority of revenue is in local currency

choices.label: C
choices.text: Management must exercise judgment considering all indicators; no single factor is determinative

choices.label: D
choices.text: The parent company may freely choose either currency regardless of indicators

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-55-5, determination of the functional currency requires management judgment when economic indicators are mixed. The standard provides six categories of indicators (cash flows, sales prices, sales markets, expenses, financing, intercompany transactions), but no single indicator is determinative. When indicators are mixed, management must weigh all factors and exercise judgment. The parent company cannot freely choose without regard to the economic substance. The correct answer is C.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary operates in Country Z. Cumulative inflation rates in Country Z over the past three years are: Year 1: 22%, Year 2: 28%, Year 3: 35%. Under ASC 830, what is the consequence of this inflation history for the subsidiary's financial statement conversion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The subsidiary's functional currency is considered to be the reporting currency (U.S. dollar), and its statements must be remeasured

choices.label: B
choices.text: The subsidiary may continue using the local currency as its functional currency with additional disclosures

choices.label: C
choices.text: The subsidiary must restate its financial statements for inflation before translating

choices.label: D
choices.text: The subsidiary must use the average rate for all balance sheet items

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-11, a highly inflationary economy is one with cumulative inflation of approximately 100% or more over a three-year period. Cumulative inflation for Country Z = (1.22 Г— 1.28 Г— 1.35) вҲ’ 1 = 2.1082 вҲ’ 1 = 1.1082 = 110.82%, which exceeds 100%. When the local economy is highly inflationary, ASC 830-10-45-11 requires that the financial statements be remeasured as if the functional currency were the reporting currency (U.S. dollar). The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent makes a long-term intercompany loan of вӮ¬5,000,000 to its German subsidiary. The loan is denominated in euros and settlement is not planned or anticipated in the foreseeable future. At year-end, the euro has depreciated against the dollar, resulting in a $200,000 exchange loss on the loan. How should the parent report this exchange loss?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In earnings as a foreign currency transaction loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: In OCI as part of the cumulative translation adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct reduction to the investment in subsidiary

choices.label: D
choices.text: The loss is not recognized until the loan is settled

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-20-35-3, foreign currency transaction gains and losses on intercompany loans that are of a long-term investment nature (i.e., settlement is not planned or anticipated in the foreseeable future) are reported in OCI as part of the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA), not in earnings. This treatment applies because such loans are considered similar in substance to equity investments in the foreign entity. The correct answer is B.

questions.stem: A U.S. company owns 100% of a foreign subsidiary with a cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) balance of negative $350,000 (loss) in AOCI. The company sells 40% of its interest in the subsidiary, reducing its ownership to 60% but retaining control. Under ASC 830, how much of the CTA is reclassified to earnings upon the partial disposal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $140,000 loss reclassified to earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: $350,000 loss reclassified to earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: $210,000 loss reclassified to earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: No amount is reclassified; CTA remains in AOCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-30-40-2 and ASC 810-10-45-23, when a parent sells a partial interest in a foreign subsidiary but retains a controlling financial interest, no portion of the CTA is reclassified from AOCI to earnings. The CTA remains in AOCI as the parent continues to consolidate the subsidiary. The difference is treated as an equity transaction (adjustment to additional paid-in capital). CTA reclassification to earnings occurs only upon a complete or substantially complete liquidation of the foreign entity, or when control is lost. The correct answer is D.

questions.subtopic: EPS - Convertible Debt with Cash Settlement
questions.stem: A company issues $10,000,000 of convertible bonds that may be settled in cash, shares, or a combination at the company's election upon conversion. The bonds are convertible into 200,000 shares. Net income is $5,000,000, interest expense on the bonds is $600,000, the tax rate is 25%, and weighted-average shares outstanding are 1,000,000. Under ASC 260, how should these bonds be reflected in diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always use the if-converted method, adding back after-tax interest and adding shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always use the treasury stock method based on excess of conversion value over par

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use the more dilutive of the if-converted method or the treasury stock method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Exclude entirely because cash settlement eliminates dilution

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-40 through 45-44, convertible instruments that may be settled in cash or shares are presumed to be settled in shares for diluted EPS purposes, and the if-converted method is applied. The numerator is adjusted by adding back after-tax interest: $600,000 Г— (1 вҲ’ 0.25) = $450,000. The denominator includes the 200,000 incremental shares. Note: Prior to ASU 2020-06, the treasury stock method could apply for cash-settled instruments, but ASU 2020-06 eliminated the treasury stock method for convertible instruments, requiring the if-converted method. The correct answer is A.

questions.subtopic: EPS - Written Put Option on Own Shares
questions.stem: A company has written a put option that gives the holder the right to sell 50,000 shares of the company's common stock to the company at $40 per share. The average market price of the stock during the period is $35. Weighted-average shares outstanding are 800,000 and net income is $2,000,000. How does this written put affect diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is anti-dilutive and excluded from diluted EPS

choices.label: B
choices.text: 7,143 additional shares are added to the denominator

choices.label: C
choices.text: 50,000 additional shares are added to the denominator

choices.label: D
choices.text: The exercise price is used instead of market price for treasury stock method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-35, written put options on the entity's own shares are dilutive when the exercise price exceeds the average market price (the option is in the money for the holder). The reverse treasury stock method applies: the company is assumed to issue shares to satisfy the put. Shares assumed repurchased with proceeds = (Exercise price Г— Shares under option) / Average market price = ($40 Г— 50,000) / $35 = 57,143 shares. Incremental shares = 57,143 вҲ’ 50,000 = 7,143 shares added to the denominator. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-35гҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҚгҖҒиҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢгғ—гғғгғҲгӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҲеЈІе»әпјүгҒҜгҖҒиЎҢдҪҝдҫЎж јгҒҢе№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгӮ’дёҠеӣһгӮӢе ҙеҗҲпјҲгғӣгғ«гғҖгғјгҒ«гҒЁгҒЈгҒҰгӮӨгғігғ»гӮ¶гғ»гғһгғҚгғјпјүгҒ«еёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„гҒЁгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮйҖҶиҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•гӮ’йҒ©з”ЁпјҡиЎҢдҪҝд»ЈйҮ‘гҒ§иІ·жҲ»гҒ—жғіе®ҡж Әж•°пјқпјҲ$40Г—50,000пјү/$35пјқ57,143ж ӘгҖӮеў—еҲҶж ӘејҸпјқ57,143вҲ’50,000пјқ7,143ж ӘгӮ’еҲҶжҜҚгҒ«иҝҪеҠ гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: In connection with a convertible debt offering, a company enters into a share lending arrangement with an investment bank, lending 100,000 shares for nominal consideration. The shares must be returned upon maturity of the convertible debt. Under ASC 260, how are the loaned shares treated for basic EPS purposes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included in the weighted-average shares outstanding for basic EPS

choices.label: B
choices.text: Excluded from the weighted-average shares outstanding for basic EPS

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included in the denominator for diluted EPS only

choices.label: D
choices.text: Treated as treasury shares and deducted from shares outstanding

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-13A, shares issued in a share lending arrangement entered into in contemplation of a convertible debt offering are excluded from the computation of basic EPS (and diluted EPS if the shares are not yet convertible) because they are mandatorily returnable. The loaned shares are not considered outstanding for EPS purposes as long as the borrower has not defaulted. The correct answer is B.

questions.stem: A company issued mandatorily convertible preferred stock on January 1, Year 1, that will automatically convert into 150,000 common shares on December 31, Year 3. The preferred stock pays an annual cumulative dividend of $300,000. For Year 1, what is the impact on basic EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Deduct the $300,000 preferred dividend from the numerator; do not include the 150,000 shares in the denominator

choices.label: B
choices.text: Do not deduct preferred dividends; include the 150,000 shares in the denominator

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deduct the $300,000 preferred dividend and include the 150,000 shares in the denominator

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment to either numerator or denominator until conversion occurs

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-12 and 260-10-45-14, mandatorily convertible instruments are not included in the weighted-average shares for basic EPS until the actual conversion date. However, the preferred dividends (whether declared or accumulated) must be deducted from the numerator when computing income available to common shareholders. The 150,000 shares are only included in the diluted EPS calculation using the if-converted method. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: Reed Corp. has net income of $1,200,000 and declares dividends of $200,000 to common shareholders and $50,000 to participating preferred shareholders. The preferred shares participate in undistributed earnings on a 1:4 ratio with common. Under the two-class method, what amount of undistributed earnings is allocated to the participating preferred shares?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Total dividends declared = $200,000 + $50,000 = $250,000. Undistributed earnings = $1,200,000 вҲ’ $250,000 = $950,000. Step 2: Participation ratio is 1:4 (preferred:common). Preferred share = 1/(1+4) = 1/5 = 20%. Step 3: Undistributed earnings allocated to preferred = $950,000 Г— 20% = $190,000. Under ASC 260-10-45-61B, the two-class method allocates undistributed earnings between common and participating securities based on their respective participation rights. The correct answer is A.

questions.subtopic: Basic EPS - Weighted Average with Buyback
questions.stem: Silo Corp. had 500,000 shares outstanding on January 1. On April 1, the company repurchased 60,000 shares. On October 1, the company issued 60,000 new shares. What is the weighted-average number of shares outstanding for the year?
questions.explanation: Weighted-average shares are computed by weighting each period's outstanding shares by the fraction of the year: Jan 1 вҖ“ Mar 31 (3 months): 500,000 Г— 3/12 = 125,000. Apr 1 вҖ“ Sep 30 (6 months): (500,000 вҲ’ 60,000) = 440,000 Г— 6/12 = 220,000. Oct 1 вҖ“ Dec 31 (3 months): (440,000 + 60,000) = 500,000 Г— 3/12 = 125,000. Total weighted-average = 125,000 + 220,000 + 125,000 = 470,000. The correct answer is C.

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Contingently Issuable Share Threshold
questions.stem: Bravo Corp. has a contingent share agreement that requires issuance of 30,000 additional common shares if cumulative revenue exceeds $50,000,000 by December 31, Year 3. As of December 31, Year 1, cumulative revenue is $38,000,000. Year 1 revenue alone is $18,000,000. Weighted-average common shares for Year 1 are 400,000. How should the contingently issuable shares be treated in Year 1 diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Include 30,000 shares because the revenue target would be met if Year 1 revenue continued

choices.label: B
choices.text: Exclude entirely because the contingency has not been met

choices.label: C
choices.text: Include a proportional 22,800 shares based on progress toward the target

choices.label: D
choices.text: Include 30,000 shares only if the effect is dilutive

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-52, contingently issuable shares are included in diluted EPS only if all necessary conditions have been satisfied as of the reporting date (i.e., the shares would be issuable if the end of the reporting period were the end of the contingency period). Since cumulative revenue of $38,000,000 does not exceed the $50,000,000 threshold, the condition is not met as of December 31, Year 1, and the shares are excluded from diluted EPS. Proportional inclusion is not permitted. The correct answer is B.

questions.subtopic: Foreign Subsidiary - Intercompany Dividend Tax Effect
questions.stem: A U.S. parent receives a dividend of LC 2,000,000 from its foreign subsidiary. The exchange rate on the dividend date is $0.75/LC. The U.S. parent has a 21% corporate tax rate and the foreign jurisdiction imposes a 5% withholding tax on dividends. Assuming no foreign tax credit limitations, what is the net after-tax dividend received by the U.S. parent in USD?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Gross dividend in USD = LC 2,000,000 Г— $0.75 = $1,500,000. Step 2: Foreign withholding tax = $1,500,000 Г— 5% = $75,000. Step 3: U.S. tax before credit = $1,500,000 Г— 21% = $315,000. Step 4: Foreign tax credit offsets U.S. tax = $75,000. Net U.S. tax = $315,000 вҲ’ $75,000 = $240,000. Step 5: Net after-tax dividend = $1,500,000 вҲ’ $75,000 вҲ’ $240,000 = $1,185,000. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: A U.S. parent owns 80% of a foreign subsidiary. The subsidiary's cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) for the year is a loss of $500,000. Under ASC 830 and ASC 810, how is the CTA allocated between the parent and noncontrolling interest (NCI)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Parent: $500,000 loss; NCI: $0

choices.label: B
choices.text: Parent: $400,000 loss; NCI: $100,000 loss

choices.label: C
choices.text: Parent: $250,000 loss; NCI: $250,000 loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: Entire $500,000 allocated to parent because CTA is a parent-level adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 810-10-45-20 and ASC 830-30-45-20, the CTA is allocated between the parent and noncontrolling interest based on their respective ownership percentages. Parent's share = 80% Г— $500,000 = $400,000 loss. NCI's share = 20% Г— $500,000 = $100,000 loss. The CTA is not solely a parent-level adjustment; it reflects the translation of the entire subsidiary's net assets and must be attributed proportionally to all shareholders. The correct answer is B.

questions.stem: Delta Corp. has basic EPS of $4.00 (net income $2,000,000 and 500,000 shares). It has three potentially dilutive securities: (1) Stock options adding 20,000 incremental shares with no numerator effect; (2) Convertible bonds adding $120,000 after-tax interest and 50,000 shares; (3) Convertible preferred adding $80,000 dividends and 30,000 shares. In what order should these securities be included when computing diluted EPS?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Options first, then convertible preferred, then convertible bonds

choices.label: B
choices.text: Convertible bonds first, then convertible preferred, then options

choices.label: C
choices.text: Options first, then convertible bonds, then convertible preferred

choices.label: D
choices.text: Convertible preferred first, then options, then convertible bonds

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-21, potentially dilutive securities must be ranked from most dilutive to least dilutive based on their incremental EPS effect (income effect divided by share effect). The per-share dilutive effects are: Options: $0 / 20,000 shares = $0.00 per share (most dilutive, always entered first). Convertible bonds: $120,000 / 50,000 shares = $2.40 per share. Convertible preferred: $80,000 / 30,000 shares = $2.67 per share (least dilutive). The correct order from most dilutive to least dilutive is: Options ($0.00), then convertible bonds ($2.40), then convertible preferred ($2.67). Each security is tested sequentially; if including a security causes EPS to increase (anti-dilutive), it is excluded. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-21гҒ«еҹәгҒҘгҒҚгҖҒжҪңеңЁзҡ„еёҢи–„еҢ–иЁјеҲёгҒҜ1ж ӘеҪ“гҒҹгӮҠеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңпјҲеҲ©зӣҠеҠ№жһңГ·ж ӘејҸеҠ№жһңпјүгҒ®еӨ§гҒҚгҒ„й ҶгҒ«дёҰгҒ№гӮӢгҖӮеҗ„иЁјеҲёгҒ®1ж ӘеҪ“гҒҹгӮҠеҠ№жһңпјҡгӮ№гғҲгғғгӮҜгӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҡ$0/20,000ж Әпјқ$0.00пјҲжңҖгӮӮеёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„гҖҒеёёгҒ«жңҖеҲқпјүгҖӮи»ўжҸӣзӨҫеӮөпјҡ$120,000/50,000ж Әпјқ$2.40гҖӮи»ўжҸӣе„Әе…Ҳж Әпјҡ$80,000/30,000ж Әпјқ$2.67пјҲжңҖгӮӮеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңгҒҢе°ҸгҒ•гҒ„пјүгҖӮжӯЈгҒ—гҒ„й ҶеәҸгҒҜпјҡгӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҲ$0.00пјүвҶ’и»ўжҸӣзӨҫеӮөпјҲ$2.40пјүвҶ’и»ўжҸӣе„Әе…Ҳж ӘпјҲ$2.67пјүгҖӮеҗ„иЁјеҲёгӮ’й Ҷж¬ЎгғҶгӮ№гғҲгҒ—гҖҒEPSгҒҢеў—еҠ пјҲеҸҚеёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„пјүгҒҷгӮӢе ҙеҗҲгҒҜйҷӨеӨ–гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is the U.S. dollar holds a net monetary asset position of LC 400,000 that remained unchanged throughout the year. The exchange rate was $0.60/LC at the beginning of the year and $0.56/LC at year-end. Under the temporal method, what remeasurement gain or loss is recognized in earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $16,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $16,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $224,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $24,000

questions.explanation: Under the temporal method, net monetary items are remeasured at the current rate, so a change in the rate produces a remeasurement gain or loss in earnings. With a net monetary ASSET position, a decline in the rate reduces the dollar value of those assets, producing a loss. Loss = LC 400,000 Г— ($0.60 вҲ’ $0.56) = LC 400,000 Г— $0.04 = $16,000 loss recognized in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: гғҶгғігғқгғ©гғ«жі•гҒ§гҒҜжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§й …зӣ®гӮ’жұәз®—ж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒгғ¬гғјгғҲеӨүеӢ•гҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡжҗҚзӣҠгҒҢзҷәз”ҹгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§иіҮз”ЈгғқгӮёгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ§гғ¬гғјгғҲгҒҢдёӢиҗҪгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгғүгғ«жҸӣз®—йЎҚгҒҢжёӣе°‘гҒ—жҗҚеӨұгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮжҗҚеӨұ = LC 400,000 Г—пјҲ$0.60 вҲ’ $0.56пјү= LC 400,000 Г— $0.04 = $16,000гӮ’еҪ“жңҹжҗҚзӣҠгҒ«иӘҚиӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Foreign Currency Transaction - Receivable Year-End Remeasurement
questions.stem: On December 1, Year 1, a U.S. company sells merchandise to a Swiss customer for CHF 300,000 on account, when the spot rate is $1.10/CHF. The receivable is still outstanding at December 31, Year 1, when the spot rate is $1.14/CHF. What foreign currency transaction gain or loss does the U.S. company recognize in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $12,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $342,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $12,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $6,000

questions.explanation: The receivable is initially recorded at CHF 300,000 Г— $1.10 = $330,000. At year-end it is remeasured at the current spot rate: CHF 300,000 Г— $1.14 = $342,000. Because the franc strengthened against the dollar, the foreign-currency receivable is worth more in dollars. Transaction gain = $342,000 вҲ’ $330,000 = $12,000 recognized in earnings under ASC 830-20-35-1.
questions.explanationJa: еЈІжҺӣйҮ‘гҒҜеҪ“еҲқ CHF 300,000 Г— $1.10 = $330,000 гҒ§иЁҲдёҠгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжңҹжң«гҒ«жұәз®—ж—ҘгӮ№гғқгғғгғҲгғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡпјҡCHF 300,000 Г— $1.14 = $342,000гҖӮгғ•гғ©гғігҒҢгғүгғ«гҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰдёҠжҳҮгҒ—гҒҹгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒеӨ–иІЁе»әеЈІжҺӣйҮ‘гҒ®гғүгғ«жҸӣз®—йЎҚгҒҢеў—еҠ гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮзӮәжӣҝе·®зӣҠ = $342,000 вҲ’ $330,000 = $12,000гӮ’еҪ“жңҹжҗҚзӣҠгҒ«иӘҚиӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: Harbor Corp. reports net income of $1,800,000 for the year. It has 400,000 shares of common stock outstanding for the entire year and 20,000 shares of 7%, $50 par cumulative preferred stock. No preferred dividends were declared during the year. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: For cumulative preferred stock, the current year's preferred dividend is subtracted from net income in computing basic EPS whether or not it is declared. Preferred dividend = 20,000 Г— $50 Г— 7% = $70,000. Income available to common = $1,800,000 вҲ’ $70,000 = $1,730,000. Basic EPS = $1,730,000 Г· 400,000 = $4.325, rounded to $4.33.
questions.explanationJa: зҙҜз©Қзҡ„е„Әе…Ҳж ӘејҸгҒ®е ҙеҗҲгҖҒеҪ“жңҹгҒ®е„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“гҒҜе®ЈиЁҖгҒ®жңүз„ЎгҒ«гҒӢгҒӢгӮҸгӮүгҒҡеҹәжң¬EPSиЁҲз®—гҒ§зҙ”еҲ©зӣҠгҒӢгӮүжҺ§йҷӨгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮе„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“ = 20,000 Г— $50 Г— 7% = $70,000гҖӮжҷ®йҖҡж Әдё»её°еұһеҲ©зӣҠ = $1,800,000 вҲ’ $70,000 = $1,730,000гҖӮеҹәжң¬EPS = $1,730,000 Г· 400,000 = $4.33гҖӮ

questions.stem: Cypress Inc. has net income of $3,000,000 and 800,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. It has $5,000,000 of 6% convertible bonds outstanding all year, each $1,000 bond convertible into 20 common shares (100,000 shares total). The tax rate is 21%. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: If-converted method: add back the after-tax interest to the numerator and the convertible shares to the denominator. Annual interest = $5,000,000 Г— 6% = $300,000. After-tax add-back = $300,000 Г— (1 вҲ’ 0.21) = $237,000. Diluted EPS = ($3,000,000 + $237,000) Г· (800,000 + 100,000) = $3,237,000 Г· 900,000 = $3.597, rounded to $3.60. Since basic EPS is $3,000,000 Г· 800,000 = $3.75, and $3.60 is lower, the bonds are dilutive.
questions.explanationJa: гӮӨгғ•гғ»гӮігғігғҗгғјгғҶгғғгғүжі•пјҡеҲҶеӯҗгҒ«зЁҺеј•еҫҢеҲ©жҒҜгҖҒеҲҶжҜҚгҒ«и»ўжҸӣж ӘејҸгӮ’еҠ з®—гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮе№ҙй–“еҲ©жҒҜ = $5,000,000 Г— 6% = $300,000гҖӮзЁҺеј•еҫҢеҠ з®—йЎҚ = $300,000 Г—пјҲ1 вҲ’ 0.21пјү= $237,000гҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = ($3,000,000 + $237,000) Г· (800,000 + 100,000) = $3,237,000 Г· 900,000 = $3.60гҖӮеҹәжң¬EPS $3.75 гӮҲгӮҠдҪҺгҒ„гҒҹгӮҒеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңгҒӮгӮҠгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Options Treasury Stock Method
questions.stem: Aspen Corp. has net income of $1,500,000 and 600,000 weighted-average shares outstanding. It has 80,000 stock options outstanding all year with an exercise price of $25. The average market price of the stock during the year was $40. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Treasury stock method: assumed proceeds = 80,000 Г— $25 = $2,000,000. Shares assumed repurchased at average market price = $2,000,000 Г· $40 = 50,000. Incremental shares = 80,000 вҲ’ 50,000 = 30,000. Diluted EPS = $1,500,000 Г· (600,000 + 30,000) = $1,500,000 Г· 630,000 = $2.381, rounded to $2.38. Because the exercise price ($25) is below the average market price ($40), the options are dilutive.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•пјҡжғіе®ҡжү•иҫјйЎҚ = 80,000 Г— $25 = $2,000,000гҖӮе№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгҒ§иІ·жҲ»гҒ—жғіе®ҡж Әж•° = $2,000,000 Г· $40 = 50,000гҖӮеў—еҲҶж ӘејҸ = 80,000 вҲ’ 50,000 = 30,000гҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = $1,500,000 Г· (600,000 + 30,000) = $1,500,000 Г· 630,000 = $2.38гҖӮиЎҢдҪҝдҫЎж ј$25 < е№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж ј$40гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒеёҢи–„еҢ–еҠ№жһңгҒӮгӮҠгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Weighted-Average Shares - Issuance Then Stock Dividend
questions.stem: Birch Co. had 600,000 shares of common stock outstanding on January 1. On April 1, it issued 120,000 additional shares for cash. On September 1, it declared a 25% stock dividend. What is the weighted-average number of shares outstanding for the year?
questions.explanation: A stock dividend is applied retroactively as if it occurred at the beginning of the period, including to shares issued for cash earlier in the year. First compute the weighted average before the dividend: (600,000 Г— 3/12) + (720,000 Г— 9/12) = 150,000 + 540,000 = 690,000. Then apply the 25% stock dividend factor: 690,000 Г— 1.25 = 862,500.
questions.explanationJa: ж ӘејҸй…ҚеҪ“гҒҜжңҹйҰ–гҒ«зҷәз”ҹгҒ—гҒҹгӮӮгҒ®гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰйҒЎеҸҠйҒ©з”ЁгҒ—гҖҒжңҹдёӯгҒ«зҸҫйҮ‘зҷәиЎҢгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹж ӘејҸгҒ«гӮӮйҒ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒҫгҒҡй…ҚеҪ“еүҚгҒ®еҠ йҮҚе№іеқҮгӮ’иЁҲз®—пјҡ(600,000 Г— 3/12) + (720,000 Г— 9/12) = 150,000 + 540,000 = 690,000гҖӮж¬ЎгҒ«25%гҒ®ж ӘејҸй…ҚеҪ“дҝӮж•°гӮ’йҒ©з”Ёпјҡ690,000 Г— 1.25 = 862,500гҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Translation - Total Assets at Current Rate
questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is its local currency reports total assets of LC 8,000,000 on its balance sheet at December 31. The year-end exchange rate is $0.45/LC, the average rate for the year was $0.48/LC, and the historical rate when the subsidiary was formed was $0.50/LC. At what dollar amount are total assets reported on the translated balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (translation), all assets and liabilities are translated at the current (year-end) exchange rate. Total assets = LC 8,000,000 Г— $0.45 = $3,600,000. The average rate is used for income statement items, and the historical rate is used only for equity accounts under translation (or for nonmonetary items under remeasurement).

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is the U.S. dollar maintains a net monetary liability position of LC 500,000 that was constant during the year. The exchange rate rose from $0.40/LC at the beginning of the year to $0.43/LC at year-end. Under the temporal method, what is the remeasurement effect on earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $15,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $215,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $215,000

questions.explanation: Under the temporal method, net monetary items are remeasured at the current rate, generating a gain or loss in earnings. With a net monetary LIABILITY position, an increase in the rate raises the dollar amount required to settle those liabilities, producing a loss. Loss = LC 500,000 Г— ($0.43 вҲ’ $0.40) = LC 500,000 Г— $0.03 = $15,000 loss in earnings.
questions.explanationJa: гғҶгғігғқгғ©гғ«жі•гҒ§гҒҜжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§й …зӣ®гӮ’жұәз®—ж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡгҒ—гҖҒжҗҚзӣҠгӮ’з”ҹгҒҳгӮӢгҖӮжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§иІ еӮөгғқгӮёгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ§гғ¬гғјгғҲгҒҢдёҠжҳҮгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒиІ еӮөжұәжёҲгҒ«еҝ…иҰҒгҒӘгғүгғ«йЎҚгҒҢеў—еҠ гҒ—жҗҚеӨұгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮжҗҚеӨұ = LC 500,000 Г—пјҲ$0.43 вҲ’ $0.40пјү= LC 500,000 Г— $0.03 = $15,000гӮ’еҪ“жңҹжҗҚзӣҠгҒ«иӘҚиӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary uses the U.S. dollar as its functional currency but keeps its books in local currency (LC). For the year: beginning inventory LC 200,000 (acquired when the rate was $0.50/LC), purchases LC 800,000 (average rate $0.48/LC), and ending inventory LC 300,000 (from recent purchases at $0.47/LC). Under the temporal method, what is remeasured cost of goods sold in U.S. dollars?
questions.explanation: Under the temporal method, cost of goods sold is remeasured using the historical rates associated with each component. Beginning inventory = LC 200,000 Г— $0.50 = $100,000. Purchases = LC 800,000 Г— $0.48 = $384,000. Ending inventory = LC 300,000 Г— $0.47 = $141,000. COGS = $100,000 + $384,000 вҲ’ $141,000 = $343,000. Applying a single current or average rate to the LC 700,000 of COGS would be incorrect under remeasurement.
questions.explanationJa: гғҶгғігғқгғ©гғ«жі•гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒеЈІдёҠеҺҹдҫЎгӮ’еҗ„ж§ӢжҲҗиҰҒзҙ гҒ«еҜҫеҝңгҒҷгӮӢеҸ–еҫ—ж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ§еҶҚжё¬е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжңҹйҰ–еңЁеә« = LC 200,000 Г— $0.50 = $100,000гҖӮд»•е…Ҙ = LC 800,000 Г— $0.48 = $384,000гҖӮжңҹжң«еңЁеә« = LC 300,000 Г— $0.47 = $141,000гҖӮCOGS = $100,000 + $384,000 вҲ’ $141,000 = $343,000гҖӮеҶҚжё¬е®ҡгҒ§гҒҜеҚҳдёҖгҒ®жұәз®—ж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲгӮ„е№іеқҮгғ¬гғјгғҲгӮ’LC 700,000гҒ®COGSгҒ«йҒ©з”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒҜиӘӨгӮҠгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, a U.S. company enters into a forward contract to buy вӮ¬1,000,000 at a forward rate of $1.20/вӮ¬. The contract is not designated as a hedge (it is held for speculation). At December 31, Year 1, the forward rate for the contract's remaining maturity is $1.23/вӮ¬. Under ASC 815, what amount of gain or loss does the company recognize on the forward contract in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No gain or loss until the contract settles

questions.explanation: A speculative (non-hedge) forward contract is a derivative carried at fair value, with changes in fair value recognized immediately in earnings under ASC 815-10-35-2. The contract locks in a purchase at $1.20/вӮ¬; the forward rate rising to $1.23/вӮ¬ means the company can buy euros below market, an asset position. Gain = вӮ¬1,000,000 Г— ($1.23 вҲ’ $1.20) = вӮ¬1,000,000 Г— $0.03 = $30,000 recognized in earnings.

questions.subtopic: Cash Flow Hedge - Forecasted Foreign Currency Purchase
questions.stem: A U.S. company designates a forward contract as a cash flow hedge of a forecasted purchase of inventory denominated in euros that is expected to occur in three months. At year-end, before the forecasted purchase occurs, the forward contract has a gain of $40,000, of which $38,000 is the effective portion. Under ASC 815, how is the effective portion reported at year-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in earnings

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recorded as a reduction of the inventory's cost at year-end

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred in other comprehensive income (OCI) until the forecasted purchase affects earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reported in the cumulative translation adjustment (CTA)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-30-35-3, for a cash flow hedge the effective portion of the gain or loss on the hedging derivative is reported initially in OCI and reclassified into earnings in the period the hedged forecasted transaction affects earnings (e.g., when the purchased inventory is sold and cost of goods sold is recognized). The effective portion is not recognized in earnings at year-end and does not go to CTA (which is reserved for translation and net investment hedges).

questions.subtopic: Basic EPS - Net Loss with Cumulative Preferred
