questions.stem: Quill Corp. reports a net loss of $400,000 for the year. It has 200,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding and 10,000 shares of cumulative preferred stock requiring an annual dividend of $60,000. No preferred dividends were declared. What is basic loss per share?
questions.explanation: Even when there is a net loss, the cumulative preferred dividend requirement is added to the loss in computing the loss attributable to common shareholders, because the preferred claim accrues regardless of declaration. Loss available to common = вҲ’$400,000 вҲ’ $60,000 = вҲ’$460,000. Basic loss per share = вҲ’$460,000 Г· 200,000 = $(2.30). The preferred dividend increases the loss per share rather than reducing income.
questions.explanationJa: зҙ”жҗҚеӨұгҒ®е ҙеҗҲгҒ§гӮӮгҖҒзҙҜз©Қзҡ„е„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“гҒ®иҰҒжұӮйЎҚгҒҜе®ЈиЁҖгҒ®жңүз„ЎгҒ«й–ўдҝӮгҒӘгҒҸзҷәз”ҹгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒжҷ®йҖҡж Әдё»её°еұһжҗҚеӨұгҒ®иЁҲз®—гҒ§жҗҚеӨұгҒ«еҠ з®—гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжҷ®йҖҡж Әдё»её°еұһжҗҚеӨұ = вҲ’$400,000 вҲ’ $60,000 = вҲ’$460,000гҖӮеҹәжң¬жҗҚеӨұ/ж Ә = вҲ’$460,000 Г· 200,000 = $(2.30)гҖӮе„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“гҒҜеҲ©зӣҠгӮ’жёӣгӮүгҒҷгҒ®гҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸ1ж ӘеҪ“гҒҹгӮҠжҗҚеӨұгӮ’жӢЎеӨ§гҒ•гҒӣгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: Ember Inc. has net income of $500,000 and 100,000 common shares outstanding all year. It has 5,000 shares of convertible preferred stock, each convertible into 4 common shares (20,000 shares total), paying an annual dividend of $8 per share ($40,000 total). What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: If-converted method: assume conversion, so the preferred dividend is not deducted and the conversion shares are added. Numerator = $500,000 (no preferred dividend deduction). Denominator = 100,000 + 20,000 = 120,000. Diluted EPS = $500,000 Г· 120,000 = $4.167, rounded to $4.17. Basic EPS = ($500,000 вҲ’ $40,000) Г· 100,000 = $4.60. Since $4.17 is lower, the convertible preferred is dilutive and is included.
questions.explanationJa: гӮӨгғ•гғ»гӮігғігғҗгғјгғҶгғғгғүжі•пјҡи»ўжҸӣгӮ’д»®е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒе„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“гҒҜжҺ§йҷӨгҒӣгҒҡгҖҒи»ўжҸӣж ӘејҸгӮ’еҠ з®—гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮеҲҶеӯҗ = $500,000пјҲе„Әе…Ҳй…ҚеҪ“жҺ§йҷӨгҒӘгҒ—пјүгҖӮеҲҶжҜҚ = 100,000 + 20,000 = 120,000гҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = $500,000 Г· 120,000 = $4.17гҖӮеҹәжң¬EPS = ($500,000 вҲ’ $40,000) Г· 100,000 = $4.60гҖӮ$4.17гҒ®ж–№гҒҢдҪҺгҒ„гҒҹгӮҒи»ўжҸӣе„Әе…Ҳж ӘгҒҜеёҢи–„еҢ–зҡ„гҒ§гҒӮгӮҠз®—е…ҘгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary (functional currency = local currency) has beginning net assets of LC 3,000,000 (beginning rate $0.70/LC). During the year it earns net income of LC 500,000 (average rate $0.66/LC) and pays dividends of LC 100,000 (rate $0.64/LC on the declaration date). The year-end rate is $0.62/LC. What is the current-year translation adjustment (CTA)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $266,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $258,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $192,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $258,000

questions.explanation: Ending net assets in LC = 3,000,000 + 500,000 вҲ’ 100,000 = LC 3,400,000. Translated at the current rate = 3,400,000 Г— $0.62 = $2,108,000. The expected USD net assets = beginning net assets at the beginning rate plus net income at the average rate minus dividends at the dividend-date rate = (3,000,000 Г— $0.70) + (500,000 Г— $0.66) вҲ’ (100,000 Г— $0.64) = $2,100,000 + $330,000 вҲ’ $64,000 = $2,366,000. CTA = actual вҲ’ expected = $2,108,000 вҲ’ $2,366,000 = вҲ’$258,000 (loss in OCI).
questions.explanationJa: жңҹжң«зҙ”иіҮз”ЈпјҲLCпјү= 3,000,000 + 500,000 вҲ’ 100,000 = LC 3,400,000гҖӮжұәз®—ж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲжҸӣз®— = 3,400,000 Г— $0.62 = $2,108,000гҖӮжңҹеҫ…USDзҙ”иіҮз”Ј = жңҹйҰ–зҙ”иіҮз”ЈпјҲжңҹйҰ–гғ¬гғјгғҲпјү+ зҙ”еҲ©зӣҠпјҲе№іеқҮгғ¬гғјгғҲпјүвҲ’ й…ҚеҪ“пјҲе®ЈиЁҖж—Ҙгғ¬гғјгғҲпјү=пјҲ3,000,000 Г— $0.70пјү+пјҲ500,000 Г— $0.66пјүвҲ’пјҲ100,000 Г— $0.64пјү= $2,100,000 + $330,000 вҲ’ $64,000 = $2,366,000гҖӮCTA = е®ҹйҡӣ вҲ’ жңҹеҫ… = $2,108,000 вҲ’ $2,366,000 = вҲ’$258,000пјҲOCIгҒ«жҗҚеӨұиЁҲдёҠпјүгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Foreign Currency Transaction - Settlement in Year 2
questions.stem: On November 1, Year 1, a U.S. company purchases goods from a French supplier for вӮ¬400,000 on account (spot rate $1.25/вӮ¬). At December 31, Year 1, the spot rate is $1.20/вӮ¬. The payable is settled on February 1, Year 2, when the spot rate is $1.22/вӮ¬. What foreign currency transaction gain or loss is recognized in Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $8,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $8,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $12,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain of $20,000

questions.explanation: At December 31, Year 1, the payable was remeasured to вӮ¬400,000 Г— $1.20 = $480,000 (carrying amount entering Year 2). On February 1, Year 2, settlement requires вӮ¬400,000 Г— $1.22 = $488,000. Because the euro strengthened from $1.20 to $1.22 during Year 2, it costs $8,000 more to settle the payable than its year-end carrying amount. Year 2 transaction loss = $488,000 вҲ’ $480,000 = $8,000. Only the change occurring in Year 2 is a Year 2 gain or loss.

questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary uses the U.S. dollar as its functional currency. It records depreciation expense of LC 100,000 on equipment that was acquired when the exchange rate was $0.80/LC. The average rate for the year is $0.74/LC and the year-end rate is $0.71/LC. Under the temporal method, at what dollar amount is depreciation expense reported?
questions.explanation: Under the temporal method (remeasurement), depreciation and amortization of nonmonetary assets are remeasured at the historical exchange rate of the related asset, because depreciation derives from the historical cost of the asset. Depreciation = LC 100,000 Г— $0.80 = $80,000. The average rate would apply only under the current rate method (translation), and the current rate is never used for this expense.

questions.subtopic: Basic EPS - Shares Issued in Business Combination
questions.stem: Cedar Corp. had 800,000 common shares outstanding on January 1. On June 1, it issued 240,000 shares to acquire a business in a purchase transaction. Net income for the year was $2,820,000. What is basic EPS?
questions.explanation: Shares issued to acquire a business in a purchase (acquisition) are included in the weighted-average from the acquisition date forward, because the acquired entity's earnings are only consolidated from that date. Weighted-average shares = (800,000 Г— 5/12) + (1,040,000 Г— 7/12) = 333,333 + 606,667 = 940,000. Basic EPS = $2,820,000 Г· 940,000 = $3.00. Unlike a stock dividend or split, acquisition shares are not applied retroactively.
questions.explanationJa: еҸ–еҫ—пјҲгғ‘гғјгғҒгӮ§гӮ№пјүгҒ§дәӢжҘӯеҸ–еҫ—гҒ®гҒҹгӮҒгҒ«зҷәиЎҢгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹж ӘејҸгҒҜгҖҒеҸ–еҫ—гҒ—гҒҹдәӢжҘӯдҪ“гҒ®еҲ©зӣҠгҒҢеҸ–еҫ—ж—Ҙд»ҘйҷҚгҒ®гҒҝйҖЈзөҗгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒеҸ–еҫ—ж—Ҙд»ҘйҷҚгҒ®еҠ йҮҚе№іеқҮгҒ«еҗ«гӮҒгӮӢгҖӮеҠ йҮҚе№іеқҮж ӘејҸж•° = (800,000 Г— 5/12) + (1,040,000 Г— 7/12) = 333,333 + 606,667 = 940,000гҖӮеҹәжң¬EPS = $2,820,000 Г· 940,000 = $3.00гҖӮж ӘејҸй…ҚеҪ“гғ»еҲҶеүІгҒЁз•°гҒӘгӮҠгҖҒеҸ–еҫ—ж ӘејҸгҒҜйҒЎеҸҠйҒ©з”ЁгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Options Plus Convertible Bonds Combined
questions.stem: Falcon Corp. has net income of $4,000,000 and 1,000,000 weighted-average common shares. Under the treasury stock method, its stock options contribute 40,000 incremental shares (no numerator effect). Its convertible bonds, if converted, would add $200,000 of after-tax interest to the numerator and 80,000 shares to the denominator. Both securities are dilutive. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: Include both dilutive securities. Numerator = $4,000,000 + $200,000 = $4,200,000. Denominator = 1,000,000 + 40,000 (options) + 80,000 (bond conversion) = 1,120,000. Diluted EPS = $4,200,000 Г· 1,120,000 = $3.75. Each security was confirmed dilutive: options alone give $4,000,000 Г· 1,040,000 = $3.85, and adding the bonds further reduces EPS to $3.75, so both are included.

questions.stem: Under ASC 830, a foreign subsidiary finances its operations primarily by issuing debt in U.S. dollars and remitting servicing of that debt to the U.S. parent, while its day-to-day local sales and costs are in local currency. According to ASC 830's financing indicator, what does this debt-financing factor suggest about the functional currency?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is an indicator pointing toward the U.S. dollar as the functional currency, weighed together with all other indicators

choices.label: B
choices.text: It conclusively establishes the local currency as the functional currency

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is irrelevant; only the currency of sales revenue matters

choices.label: D
choices.text: It requires the parent to designate a third stable currency

questions.explanation: ASC 830-10-55-5 lists financing as one of the economic indicators of the functional currency. Debt denominated and serviced in the parent's currency (USD) is a financing indicator pointing toward the dollar as the functional currency. However, no single indicator is determinative; management weighs financing together with cash flow, sales price, sales market, expense, and intercompany indicators to reach an overall conclusion.

questions.subtopic: Functional Currency - Economy Ceases to be Highly Inflationary
questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary's local economy had been highly inflationary, so the parent remeasured its statements using the U.S. dollar as the functional currency. The cumulative three-year inflation rate has now fallen below 100%, and the economy is no longer considered highly inflationary. Under ASC 830, how should the parent account for this change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Retroactively restate all prior periods as if the local currency had always been the functional currency

choices.label: B
choices.text: Continue using the U.S. dollar as the functional currency indefinitely once it has been adopted

choices.label: C
choices.text: Restate the reporting-currency (USD) amounts of nonmonetary items into the local currency at current rates to establish the new functional-currency accounting bases, then translate prospectively

choices.label: D
choices.text: Immediately reclassify all prior remeasurement gains and losses from earnings into OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-10-45-15 and 45-16, when an economy ceases to be highly inflationary, the entity restates the functional-currency (formerly reporting-currency, i.e., USD) amounts of nonmonetary assets and liabilities into the new functional currency (the local currency) using current exchange rates at the date of change. Those restated amounts become the new accounting bases, and the entity translates prospectively using the current rate method. Prior periods are not retroactively restated.

questions.subtopic: Net Investment Hedge - Nonderivative Foreign Currency Debt
questions.stem: A U.S. parent has a net investment in a euro-functional-currency subsidiary. To hedge this exposure, the parent designates a вӮ¬5,000,000 euro-denominated bank loan (a nonderivative instrument) as a hedge of the net investment. During the year the euro weakens, producing a $150,000 transaction gain on the euro loan, and the hedge is fully effective. Under ASC 815, how is the $150,000 gain on the loan reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In current period earnings as a foreign currency transaction gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a direct increase to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: In the cumulative translation adjustment within OCI

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deferred and recognized only when the loan is repaid

questions.explanation: Under ASC 815-35-35-1, a nonderivative financial instrument such as foreign-currency-denominated debt may be designated as a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation. The effective portion of the transaction gain or loss on the hedging instrument is reported in the cumulative translation adjustment within OCI, offsetting the translation adjustment on the net investment. Here the entire $150,000 gain (fully effective) is reported in CTA, not in earnings.

questions.subtopic: Remeasurement - Net Monetary Exposure with Activity
questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary (functional currency = U.S. dollar) begins the year with net monetary assets of LC 1,000,000 (beginning rate $0.30/LC). During the year, operations increase net monetary assets by LC 500,000, earned evenly at the average rate of $0.28/LC. Net monetary assets at year-end are LC 1,500,000, and the year-end rate is $0.25/LC. Under the temporal method, what remeasurement gain or loss is recognized in earnings?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Loss of $75,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of $65,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain of $65,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Loss of $125,000

questions.explanation: The remeasurement gain or loss equals the actual year-end dollar value of net monetary items minus the dollar value expected from translating the beginning balance and the period's change at the rates that applied. Expected = (LC 1,000,000 Г— $0.30) + (LC 500,000 Г— $0.28) = $300,000 + $140,000 = $440,000. Actual = LC 1,500,000 Г— $0.25 = $375,000. Remeasurement loss = $440,000 вҲ’ $375,000 = $65,000 in earnings, reflecting the decline in the rate applied to net monetary asset exposure.
questions.explanationJa: еҶҚжё¬е®ҡжҗҚзӣҠгҒҜгҖҒжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§й …зӣ®гҒ®е№ҙжң«е®ҹйҡӣгғүгғ«дҫЎеҖӨгҒӢгӮүгҖҒжңҹйҰ–ж®Ӣй«ҳгҒЁжңҹдёӯеӨүеӢ•гӮ’гҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢгҒ®гғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ§жҸӣз®—гҒ—гҒҹжңҹеҫ…гғүгғ«дҫЎеҖӨгӮ’е·®гҒ—еј•гҒ„гҒҹйЎҚгҒ«зӯүгҒ—гҒ„гҖӮжңҹеҫ… = (LC 1,000,000 Г— $0.30) + (LC 500,000 Г— $0.28) = $300,000 + $140,000 = $440,000гҖӮе®ҹйҡӣ = LC 1,500,000 Г— $0.25 = $375,000гҖӮеҶҚжё¬е®ҡжҗҚеӨұ = $440,000 вҲ’ $375,000 = $65,000гӮ’еҪ“жңҹжҗҚзӣҠгҒ«иӘҚиӯҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжӯЈе‘іиІЁе№ЈжҖ§иіҮз”ЈгӮЁгӮҜгӮ№гғқгғјгӮёгғЈгғјгҒ«йҒ©з”ЁгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгғ¬гғјгғҲгҒ®дёӢиҗҪгӮ’еҸҚжҳ гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Two-Class Method - Common EPS with Declared and Undistributed Earnings
questions.stem: Vista Corp. has net income of $2,000,000. It declares $300,000 of dividends to common shareholders and $100,000 of dividends to participating preferred shareholders. There are 200,000 common shares and 50,000 participating preferred shares that share in undistributed earnings on a 1:1 per-share basis with common. Under the two-class method, what is basic EPS for common shareholders?
questions.explanation: Two-class method: (1) Undistributed earnings = $2,000,000 вҲ’ $300,000 вҲ’ $100,000 = $1,600,000. (2) Allocate undistributed earnings on a per-share basis: common share = 200,000 Г· (200,000 + 50,000) = 80%, so common gets $1,600,000 Г— 80% = $1,280,000. (3) Total earnings to common = declared common dividends $300,000 + undistributed $1,280,000 = $1,580,000. (4) Basic EPS = $1,580,000 Г· 200,000 = $7.90.
questions.explanationJa: 2гӮҜгғ©гӮ№жі•пјҡв‘ жңӘеҲҶй…ҚеҲ©зӣҠ = $2,000,000 вҲ’ $300,000 вҲ’ $100,000 = $1,600,000гҖӮв‘ЎжңӘеҲҶй…ҚеҲ©зӣҠгӮ’1ж ӘеҪ“гҒҹгӮҠгҒ§й…ҚеҲҶпјҡжҷ®йҖҡж ӘеүІеҗҲ = 200,000 Г· (200,000 + 50,000) = 80%гҖҒжҷ®йҖҡж Әй…ҚеҲҶ = $1,600,000 Г— 80% = $1,280,000гҖӮв‘ўжҷ®йҖҡж ӘгҒёгҒ®еҲ©зӣҠеҗҲиЁҲ = е®ЈиЁҖжёҲжҷ®йҖҡй…ҚеҪ“$300,000 + жңӘеҲҶй…Қ$1,280,000 = $1,580,000гҖӮв‘Јеҹәжң¬EPS = $1,580,000 Г· 200,000 = $7.90гҖӮ

questions.subtopic: Translation - Sales Revenue at Average Rate
questions.stem: A foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is its local currency reports sales revenue of LC 6,000,000 earned evenly throughout the year. The weighted-average rate for the year is $0.33/LC, the year-end rate is $0.31/LC, and the beginning-of-year rate is $0.35/LC. At what dollar amount is sales revenue reported on the translated income statement?
questions.explanation: Under the current rate method (translation), income statement items are translated at the rate on the dates they are recognized; for items earned evenly, the weighted-average rate for the period is used. Sales revenue = LC 6,000,000 Г— $0.33 = $1,980,000. The year-end rate is used only for balance sheet items, and the beginning rate is not used for current-year revenue.

questions.subtopic: Foreign Currency - Unhedged Firm Purchase Commitment
questions.stem: On December 15, Year 1, a U.S. company enters into a noncancelable purchase commitment to buy machinery from a Japanese vendor for ВҘ50,000,000, with delivery and payment to occur on March 31, Year 2. The commitment is not hedged. Under ASC 830, how should the U.S. company account for the commitment in its December 31, Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record a payable of the yen amount remeasured at the December 31, Year 1 spot rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the machinery and a payable at the December 15, Year 1 spot rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a foreign currency gain or loss in earnings for the rate change from December 15 to December 31, Year 1

choices.label: D
choices.text: No transaction is recorded at December 31, Year 1; the purchase and related payable are recognized when the machinery is received

questions.explanation: An unhedged firm purchase commitment is an executory contract. Under ASC 830-20-25-1, a foreign currency transaction is recorded only when the transaction is recognized in the accounts вҖ” generally when the goods are received and title passes. Because the machinery is not delivered until March 31, Year 2, no asset, payable, or foreign currency gain or loss is recorded at December 31, Year 1. The commitment may require disclosure but is not recognized.

questions.subtopic: Diluted EPS - Treasury Stock Method Uses Average Price
questions.stem: Maple Corp. has net income of $2,400,000 and 800,000 weighted-average shares outstanding. It has 60,000 stock options outstanding all year with an exercise price of $30. The average market price during the year was $40, and the year-end (closing) market price was $48. What is diluted EPS?
questions.explanation: The treasury stock method uses the AVERAGE market price for the period (not the year-end price) to compute assumed repurchases. Proceeds = 60,000 Г— $30 = $1,800,000. Shares repurchased = $1,800,000 Г· $40 (average) = 45,000. Incremental shares = 60,000 вҲ’ 45,000 = 15,000. Diluted EPS = $2,400,000 Г· (800,000 + 15,000) = $2,400,000 Г· 815,000 = $2.945, rounded to $2.94. Using the $48 year-end price would understate the incremental shares and is incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: иҮӘе·ұж ӘејҸжі•гҒ§гҒҜгҖҒиІ·жҲ»гҒ—жғіе®ҡгҒ®иЁҲз®—гҒ«жңҹжң«дҫЎж јгҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸжңҹй–“гҒ®е№іеқҮеёӮе ҙдҫЎж јгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮжү•иҫјйЎҚ = 60,000 Г— $30 = $1,800,000гҖӮиІ·жҲ»гҒ—ж Әж•° = $1,800,000 Г· $40пјҲе№іеқҮпјү= 45,000гҖӮеў—еҲҶж ӘејҸ = 60,000 вҲ’ 45,000 = 15,000гҖӮеёҢи–„еҢ–EPS = $2,400,000 Г· (800,000 + 15,000) = $2,400,000 Г· 815,000 = $2.94гҖӮжңҹжң«дҫЎж ј$48гӮ’дҪҝгҒҶгҒЁеў—еҲҶж ӘејҸгӮ’йҒҺе°Ҹи©•дҫЎгҒҷгӮӢгҒҹгӮҒиӘӨгӮҠгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ

questions.subtopic: CTA - Loss of Control via Deconsolidation
questions.stem: A U.S. company owns 100% of a foreign subsidiary with a cumulative translation adjustment (CTA) balance of a $420,000 gain in AOCI. The company distributes the entire subsidiary to its own shareholders in a spin-off, losing control of (deconsolidating) the subsidiary. Under ASC 830 and ASC 810, how is the CTA accounted for upon deconsolidation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The $420,000 CTA gain remains in AOCI because no cash was received

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $420,000 CTA gain is transferred directly to retained earnings without affecting earnings

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entire $420,000 CTA gain is reclassified from AOCI into earnings as part of the gain or loss on deconsolidation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only a pro-rata portion of the CTA is reclassified based on the spin-off's fair value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-30-40-1 and ASC 810-10-40-3A, a loss of a controlling financial interest in a foreign subsidiary вҖ” including a deconsolidation through a spin-off вҖ” triggers full reclassification of the related CTA from AOCI into earnings as part of the gain or loss recognized on deconsolidation. The full $420,000 gain is released to earnings. The absence of cash consideration does not change this treatment, because deconsolidation is the triggering event, not the receipt of cash.
